Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on the overall podium of the Tour, 3rd of an edition dominated by France’s Roger Lapébie.
Champagnole also hosted a start in 1964 (Jan Janssen won in Thonon-les-Bains) and another finish in 2020, after 166.5 km from Bourg-en-Bresse. Soren Kragh Andersen attacked in the finale to take his second stage win in this Tour, the 21st for Denmark. The nation is up to 30 stage wins after Mads Pedersen’s victory in Foix last week.
Visited by the Tour de l’Avenir on several occasions, Champagnole was notably on the map in 2021, giving way to stage 6 en route to Septmoncel, where twins Anders and Tobias Johannessen achieved a spectacular 1-2.
Most recently, Champagnole was the start town of stage 7 of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024. The polka-dot jersey on her back, Justine Ghekiere attacked early in the stage and powered to a solo victory at Le Grand-Bornand. Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney retained the Maillot Jaune on the eve of her exhilarating triumph at L’Alpe Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on the overall podium of the Tour, 3rd of an edition dominated by France’s Roger Lapébie.
Champagnole also hosted a start in 1964 (Jan Janssen won in Thonon-les-Bains) and another finish in 2020, after 166.5 km from Bourg-en-Bresse. Soren Kragh Andersen attacked in the finale to take his second stage win in this Tour, the 21st for Denmark. The nation is up to 30 stage wins after Mads Pedersen’s victory in Foix last week.
Visited by the Tour de l’Avenir on several occasions, Champagnole was notably on the map in 2021, giving way to stage 6 en route to Septmoncel, where twins Anders and Tobias Johannessen achieved a spectacular 1-2.
Most recently, Champagnole was the start town of stage 7 of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024. The polka-dot jersey on her back, Justine Ghekiere attacked early in the stage and powered to a solo victory at Le Grand-Bornand. Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney retained the Maillot Jaune on the eve of her exhilarating triumph at L’Alpe Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on the overall podium of the Tour, 3rd of an edition dominated by France’s Roger Lapébie.
Champagnole also hosted a start in 1964 (Jan Janssen won in Thonon-les-Bains) and another finish in 2020, after 166.5 km from Bourg-en-Bresse. Soren Kragh Andersen attacked in the finale to take his second stage win in this Tour, the 21st for Denmark. The nation is up to 30 stage wins after Mads Pedersen’s victory in Foix last week.
Visited by the Tour de l’Avenir on several occasions, Champagnole was notably on the map in 2021, giving way to stage 6 en route to Septmoncel, where twins Anders and Tobias Johannessen achieved a spectacular 1-2.
Most recently, Champagnole was the start town of stage 7 of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024. The polka-dot jersey on her back, Justine Ghekiere attacked early in the stage and powered to a solo victory at Le Grand-Bornand. Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney retained the Maillot Jaune on the eve of her exhilarating triumph at L’Alpe Since the Grand Départ in Barcelona two weeks ago, Jonas Vingegaard has indeed been wearing an FFP2 mask before and after every stage. The Danish and his team Visma–Lease a Bike, cite a "cautious and precautionary approach." Belgium’s Sylvère Maes, the overall winner the year before, won an ITT that started in Lons-le-Saunier. In the afternoon, the race went from Champagnole to Genève, and the reigning Swiss champion Léo Amberg took the stage. A few weeks later, he was the first Swiss to stand on the overall podium of the Tour, 3rd of an edition dominated by France’s Roger Lapébie.
Champagnole also hosted a start in 1964 (Jan Janssen won in Thonon-les-Bains) and another finish in 2020, after 166.5 km from Bourg-en-Bresse. Soren Kragh Andersen attacked in the finale to take his second stage win in this Tour, the 21st for Denmark. The nation is up to 30 stage wins after Mads Pedersen’s victory in Foix last week.
Visited by the Tour de l’Avenir on several occasions, Champagnole was notably on the map in 2021, giving way to stage 6 en route to Septmoncel, where twins Anders and Tobias Johannessen achieved a spectacular 1-2.
Most recently, Champagnole was the start town of stage 7 of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024. The polka-dot jersey on her back, Justine Ghekiere attacked early in the stage and powered to a solo victory at Le Grand-Bornand. Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney retained the Maillot Jaune on the eve of her exhilarating triumph at L’Alpe d’Huez."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-19T12:48:09+02:00","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"7d969d07935fcbf998f72604ac6c7e7da7569692c0a48fa752595b94f8739821","_bind":"publication_en-2026-15","_origin":"news-2026-15","_updatedAt":1784458223615,"_parent":"stage-2026:7e9e83501c166a930fe23675c3abfad207c89df1acebba7eea1f0e2825d39385","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:champagnole-maes-johannessens-ghekiere","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","slug":"la-roche-sur-foron-km-158-7-from-mottet-to-kwiatkowski","type":"liv","id":1349285,"updatedBy":"bvittek","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":15,"createdAt":"2026-07-19T16:47:01+02:00","title":"La Roche-sur-Foron (km 158.7): from Mottet to Kwiatkowski","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-19T16:47:01+02:00","year":2026,"text":["Home a stage start of the Critérium du Dauphiné in 1988, La Roche-sur-Foron cheered for Charly Mottet, overall leader of the race, before the peloton headed to Chambéry, where Acacio Da Silva won the stage ahead of Mariano Sanchez and Charly Mottet, who retained the leader’s jersey.
Cycling enthusiasts mostly associate La Roche-sur-Foron with Michael Kwiatkoswki’s success in the Tour de France 2020. In that edition, stage 18 started from Méribel and the Polish star made the breakaway with Richard Carapaz, riding for Ineos Grenadiers at the time. The British managed to join a 24-rider group, which includes his teammate Brent Van Moer, by following some attacks. The Dutchman Thymen Arensman (Netcompany Ineos) triumphed at Luchon-Superbagnères exactly one year ago to the day, before securing a second victory at La Plagne six days later.

The entire family is involved in the local cycling scene: their father manages the VC Annemasse, and their sister Maeva is also a cyclist!
Annemasse is next to Geneva. Tour des Pays de Savoie (in 2012) and Tour de l’Avenir (2014) first ventured there before the Critérium du Dauphiné, now Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, concluded on these slopes in 2017, 2022, and 2026.
When the ascent first appeared in the Critérium du Dauphiné, in 2017, it turned the race upside down with Denmark’s Jakob Fuglsang seizing the stage win and the final overall lead.
Five years later, it was his compatriot Jonas Vingegaard (Visma-Lease a Bike) who left his mark on the Plateau de Solaison just a few weeks before he took his first Tour de France.
